backlog.title: Production Bug Fixes — Offline Guarantee + Polish
backlog.description: Fix the 1 Critical defect (runtime network calls violating PRD §1 + §7.8 '100% local') and 3 Minor defects (status.sh exit code, mic probe TTL, install.sh offline confirmation) identified by end-to-end creative QA. All fixes are small, surgical, and grounded in the existing codebase patterns.

milestones.title: Critical Offline Guarantee (Issue 1 + Issue 4)
milestones.description: Eliminate all runtime network calls to huggingface.co by exporting HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 and TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1 in the sanctioned daemon launch wrapper (launch_daemon.sh). Add regression guards (static drift-guard + close the circular-proof gap in test_idle_and_gpu.sh). Confirm via install.sh post-restart assertion. This is the single most important fix: it restores the project's headline '100% local, nothing sent to a cloud' promise.

tasks.title: Add offline env exports + regression guards to launch wrapper
tasks.description: Export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 and TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1 in voice_typing/launch_daemon.sh before the exec. Add a static drift-guard test (pytest) asserting the wrapper contains the exports. Close the circular-proof gap in tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh by removing the test's own pre-set of these vars and adding a journal grep assertion that no HTTP requests appear after startup.

subtasks.title: Export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 and TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1 in launch_daemon.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_61d807f18dbe/bugfix/001_18d83a3a5991/architecture/research_hf_offline.md and scout_launch_status_install.md. HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 is the authoritative flag that makes huggingface_hub read exclusively from the local cache (~/.cache/huggingface/hub) with zero HTTP — it skips the freshness GET request to https://huggingface.co that currently fires on every daemon startup (2 requests per startup, one per model, proven in the live journal). TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1 is harmless belt-and-suspenders (faster-whisper has no transformers dependency, but it costs nothing). Both vars are read at import time by huggingface_hub/constants.py, so they MUST be in the environment BEFORE python starts — the export-before-exec pattern is strictly more correct than setting os.environ inside Python.
2. INPUT: voice_typing/launch_daemon.sh is a bash wrapper (#!/usr/bin/env bash, set -euo pipefail, 60 lines). It already exports LD_LIBRARY_PATH (line 53) before the final exec at line 60: `exec "$PY" -m voice_typing.daemon "$@"`. The systemd unit (systemd/voice-typing.service) has NO Environment= directives by design (the comment at line 29 says 'DO NOT add Environment=LD_LIBRARY_PATH=...') — launch_daemon.sh is the sanctioned single source for the daemon process environment.
3. LOGIC: Insert two export lines between line 58 (the `fi` closing the CUDA-libs if/else) and line 60 (the exec). The insertion MUST be before the exec (env vars are read at process exec via execve). Add a clear comment explaining WHY (offline guarantee per PRD §1 + §7.8; models prefetched at install time by prefetch.py; HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 skips HF freshness checks; uncached model = fail-fast LocalEntryNotFoundError by design). The exact lines to add:
   export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1
   export TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1
4. OUTPUT: launch_daemon.sh now exports both offline vars before exec. The daemon (and its multiprocessing.spawn children — RealtimeSTT workers) inherit them. Verify with: `bash -n voice_typing/launch_daemon.sh` (syntax check) and a manual `voice_typing/launch_daemon.sh` start followed by `journalctl --user -u voice-typing -n 20 | grep 'HTTP Request'` → expect ZERO lines.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Add a comment block in launch_daemon.sh explaining the offline exports (the file's header already documents the LD_LIBRARY_PATH pattern — follow that style). No external docs file changes here (README already claims 'Fully-local voice typing' and 'nothing is sent to a cloud' — the fix makes those claims true, no README edit needed for this subtask).

subtasks.title: Add static drift-guard test asserting launch_daemon.sh exports offline vars
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_61d807f18dbe/bugfix/001_18d83a3a5991/architecture/scout_launch_status_install.md §5. tests/test_systemd_unit.py already has a pattern for static text-grep drift guards: it reads a file as text, filters to non-comment lines via `_unit_lines()`, and asserts directives. The new test mirrors this pattern but greps launch_daemon.sh instead of the systemd unit. This is a FAST pytest test (no CUDA, no models, no network) that catches the regression if someone removes the exports.
2. INPUT: tests/test_systemd_unit.py (95 lines) contains `_unit_path()` → resolves systemd/voice-typing.service via `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / 'systemd' / 'voice-typing.service'`. The new test needs a similar path helper for launch_daemon.sh: `Path(__file__).resolve().parent.parent / 'voice_typing' / 'launch_daemon.sh'`. The wrapper file (from P1.M1.T1.S1) now contains `export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1` and `export TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1` before the `exec "$PY"` line.
3. LOGIC: Add a new test function to tests/test_systemd_unit.py (e.g. `test_launch_daemon_exports_offline_vars`). Read launch_daemon.sh as text. Assert: (a) the file contains `export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1` (exact match, could be `export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1` or with quotes — accept both `=1` and `="1"` and `='1'`); (b) the file contains `export TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1`; (c) both export lines appear BEFORE the `exec "$PY"` line (line-number check: find the line indices and assert exports come first). Follow the existing test style: descriptive docstring explaining the PRD §1 + §7.8 rationale, why this is a static check (drift guard, no live systemd needed), and what regression it prevents.
4. OUTPUT: A new pytest test in tests/test_systemd_unit.py that passes when the exports exist and fails if they're removed. Run: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_systemd_unit.py -v`. All existing tests + the new one must pass.
5. DOCS: none — no user-facing/config/API surface change. This is a test-only subtask.

subtasks.title: Close the circular-proof gap in test_idle_and_gpu.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_61d807f18dbe/bugfix/001_18d83a3a5991/architecture/scout_launch_status_install.md §6. tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh line 206 currently does `export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1` BEFORE launching the daemon via `"$LAUNCH"` (launch_daemon.sh) at line 207. This MASKS the production defect: the test proves the daemon CAN run offline, but NOT that the PRODUCTION path (systemd → launch_daemon.sh with no pre-set env) runs offline. The PRD explicitly calls this 'a test that does NOT pre-set HF_HUB_OFFLINE itself but instead invokes the real launch_daemon.sh' as the recommended regression guard. After P1.M1.T1.S1, launch_daemon.sh exports the vars, so removing the test's pre-set still results in the daemon running offline — but now the test exercises the REAL production path.
2. INPUT: tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh is a heavy bash integration test (~400 lines, manual, requires CUDA + prefetched models + ~3-4 min runtime). It launches the daemon at line 207: `XDG_CONFIG_HOME="$WORK/config" "$LAUNCH" > "$WORK/daemon.log" 2>&1 &`. The daemon log is captured in `$WORK/daemon.log`. Line 206 (`export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1`) is the masking line. The test's G-OFFLINE invariant is documented at lines 51-52. The criterion-8 assertion is at lines 386-388. After P1.M1.T1.S1, launch_daemon.sh exports both vars before exec, so the daemon inherits them even without the test's pre-set.
3. LOGIC: (a) Remove line 206's `export HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1` (or comment it out with an explanation that launch_daemon.sh now provides them). (b) Update the G-OFFLINE comment (lines 51-52, 202-205) to explain the NEW approach: the test no longer pre-sets offline vars; launch_daemon.sh exports them; the test now exercises the REAL production path. (c) Add a post-startup assertion that greps `$WORK/daemon.log` for `HTTP Request: GET https://huggingface.co` and FAILS if any are found — this is the actual regression guard. Place it after the daemon is confirmed ready (after the ready-wait loop, ~line 215+). Example: `if grep -q 'HTTP Request: GET https://huggingface.co' "$WORK/daemon.log"; then die 'FAIL: daemon made network calls to huggingface.co (offline exports missing from launch_daemon.sh?)'; fi`. (d) Update the criterion-8 PASS message (lines 386-388) to reflect that the proof is now via the journal grep (no HTTP requests found), not via pre-setting the env vars. (e) Update line 404's `offline_env:` line to reflect the new approach. IMPORTANT: the test is NOT collected by pytest (it's a manual bash script). Do NOT make it require network — if the exports are missing, the daemon should fail to start (LocalEntryNotFoundError if offline + uncached), which the ready-wait timeout catches.
4. OUTPUT: tests/test_idle_and_gpu.sh no longer pre-sets HF_HUB_OFFLINE; it relies on launch_daemon.sh's exports and greps the daemon log for zero HTTP requests. The criterion-8 assertion is now a real proof of production-path offline behavior, not a circular proof. NOTE: this test requires CUDA + models to actually run, so it cannot be verified in CI — the static drift-guard test (P1.M1.T1.S2) is the CI safety net.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the test file's own header comments (G-OFFLINE invariant, criterion-8 section) to document the new non-circular proof approach. No external docs files.

tasks.title: install.sh offline confirmation (Issue 4)
tasks.description: Add a post-restart journal grep assertion to install.sh that verifies the deployed daemon makes no network calls to huggingface.co, and add a summary line confirming the daemon runs fully offline. This closes the loop on the documented '100% local' promise at install time.

subtasks.title: Add post-restart journal grep + offline summary line to install.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_61d807f18dbe/bugfix/001_18d83a3a5991/architecture/scout_launch_status_install.md §4. install.sh restarts the daemon at line 116 (`systemctl --user restart voice-typing.service`), then checks `is-active` at lines 142-146, then prints the summary at step [7/7] (~line 149). The PRD Issue 4 says: 'Optionally add a post-install journal grep in install.sh that asserts no HTTP Request: GET https://huggingface.co lines appear after the first restart.' And: 'a line in install.sh's summary confirming daemon runs fully offline would close the loop on the documented promise.' This subtask depends on P1.M1.T1.S1 (the wrapper must export the vars for the grep to pass).
2. INPUT: install.sh is a bash script (~165 lines, set -euo pipefail). After step [4/7] (line 116: `systemctl --user restart voice-typing.service`), systemd restarts the daemon via launch_daemon.sh (which now exports HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1). The daemon writes to journald (stderr → journald per the no_log_file=True kwarg). The is-active check is at lines 142-146. The summary block starts at line 149 (`echo '==> [7/7] done — voice-typing installed'`).
3. LOGIC: (a) After the restart (line 116) and a brief sleep for model load (~3-5 seconds, matching the daemon's startup time), grep the journal for HTTP requests: `sleep 5; if journalctl --user -u voice-typing --since '1 min ago' --no-pager 2>/dev/null | grep -q 'HTTP Request: GET https://huggingface.co'; then echo 'install.sh: WARNING — daemon made network calls to huggingface.co after restart; offline exports may be missing.' >&2; fi`. Make this a WARNING (not fatal) so a failed grep doesn't abort install.sh under set -e — the static test (P1.M1.T1.S2) is the hard gate. (b) In the summary block (step [7/7]), add a line like: `echo 'offline: daemon runs fully local (HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 via launch_daemon.sh) — no network at runtime'`. This mirrors the existing summary style (CUDA verdict, daemon status lines).
4. OUTPUT: install.sh now asserts (warn-level) no network calls after restart and confirms offline operation in the summary. The warning surfaces a regression if the exports are removed.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] install.sh's stdout IS the user-facing install/usage quick-start (per its header comment: 'This script's stdout IS the user-facing install/usage quick-start (Mode A docs)'). The new summary line IS the doc update — it confirms the offline guarantee to the user at install time.

milestones.title: Minor Polish — status.sh Exit Code + Mic Probe TTL (Issues 2 + 3)
milestones.description: Fix two independent minor defects: (1) status.sh exits non-zero on missing/corrupt state.json, violating its own documented 'exit 0 (never abort)' contract; (2) the PyAudio mic probe runs on every arm while holding the daemon lock (~40 ms per arm), causing perceptible latency and lock contention under concurrent load. Both fixes are small and surgical.

tasks.title: Fix status.sh exit-code contract (Issue 2)
tasks.description: Add an explicit exit 0 to voice_typing/status.sh after the jq call so the script honors its documented 'must print an empty line with exit 0 (never abort)' contract. Add a regression test that runs status.sh against missing and corrupt state.json, asserting exit 0.

subtasks.title: Add exit 0 + soften comment + regression test for status.sh
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_61d807f18dbe/bugfix/001_18d83a3a5991/architecture/scout_launch_status_install.md §1 and §7. voice_typing/status.sh is a POSIX sh script (#!/bin/sh, 39 lines) with NO `set -e` and NO `exit` command. Its exit status is jq's exit status (the last command, lines 34-39). When state.json is missing, jq exits 2; when corrupt, jq exits 5. The header comment (lines 23-25) promises 'exit 0 (never abort)' — that contract is violated. There is NO existing test for status.sh (grep found only a comment reference in test_feedback.py:351). tmux's #(...) substitution ignores exit codes, so the practical impact is negligible for tmux — but the documented contract is broken and a non-tmux caller checking $? would be misled.
2. INPUT: voice_typing/status.sh — the jq call ends at line 39: `' "$STATE" 2>/dev/null`. The script reads STATE from `${XDG_RUNTIME_DIR:-/run/user/$(id -u)}/voice-typing/state.json`. The header comment at lines 23-25 states the 'exit 0 (never abort)' contract and claims 'otherwise tmux would show an error string in status-right' (which is overstated — tmux ignores exit codes in #(...)).
3. LOGIC: (a) Append `exit 0` as a new line after line 39 (after the jq call). This makes the script always exit 0 regardless of jq's exit code, honoring the documented contract. (b) Optionally soften the comment at line 24: change 'otherwise tmux would show an error string in status-right' to note that tmux's #(...) ignores exit codes, but the explicit exit 0 honors the documented contract for any caller (tmux or non-tmux). (c) Add a new regression test. Since there's no existing status.sh test, create a new pytest file tests/test_status_sh.py (or add to an existing test file). The test should: create a temp XDG_RUNTIME_DIR, run status.sh with NO state file → assert exit code 0 AND empty stdout; write corrupt JSON to state.json → run status.sh → assert exit code 0 AND empty stdout. Use subprocess.run to invoke the script with a controlled XDG_RUNTIME_DIR env var. Assert `result.returncode == 0` and `result.stdout.strip() == ''` in both cases.
4. OUTPUT: status.sh now always exits 0. A new test proves it for both missing and corrupt state.json. Run: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_status_sh.py -v` (all pass). Also verify: `XDG_RUNTIME_DIR=$(mktemp -d) voice_typing/status.sh; echo $?` → prints `0`.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The status.sh header comment IS the user-facing doc (it documents the tmux integration and the exit-code contract). The comment softening (step b) updates that doc in-place. No external docs files.

tasks.title: Cache PyAudio mic probe with TTL (Issue 3)
tasks.description: Add a TTL cache to _refresh_mic_status() in voice_typing/daemon.py so the expensive PyAudio probe (import pyaudio; PyAudio(); enumerate; terminate — ~40 ms) does not run on every arm while holding the daemon lock. Re-probe at most once every 30 seconds. This preserves the mic-health surface in voicectl status while eliminating the per-arm cost.

subtasks.title: Implement TTL cache in _refresh_mic_status + update mic probe tests
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/002_61d807f18dbe/bugfix/001_18d83a3a5991/architecture/scout_mic_probe.md and issue_analysis.md §Issue 3. The full probe path: _arm() (daemon.py:575-581) → _refresh_mic_status() (daemon.py:633-648) → prober (production: _probe_mic at 650-672). _arm() is called under self._lock by start()/toggle(). The probe does `import pyaudio; pa = pyaudio.PyAudio(); <enumerate all devices>; pa.terminate()` — ~39-43 ms, every arm. ALL _arm/_disarm/_refresh_mic_status calls happen under self._lock (or in __init__, single-threaded), so the TTL cache fields are lock-protected for free — NO extra locking needed. The existing MicRetryRateLimitFilter (daemon.py:1106-1123) is the structural analog: it uses a `time.monotonic()` window with a `0.0 == never` sentinel. `import time` is already at daemon.py:76. Tests use `_fixed_clock(monkeypatch, t)` (test_daemon.py:1551-1553) to freeze `daemon.time.monotonic` deterministically. The `_make_daemon()` factory (test_daemon.py:424-430) injects `mic_prober=_ok_probe`. Counting probers are the existing idiom (test_daemon.py:1500, 1511).
2. INPUT: (a) daemon.py __init__ (lines 450-456) sets up `self._mic_ok`, `self._mic_error`, `self._mic_prober`, then calls `self._refresh_mic_status()`. (b) _refresh_mic_status() (lines 633-648) is the SINGLE writer of _mic_ok/_mic_error and the SINGLE sanctioned caller of the probe. (c) _arm() (line 581) calls `self._refresh_mic_status()` unconditionally. (d) The existing test `test_arm_refreshes_mic_status` (test_daemon.py:1506) asserts `len(calls) == 2` after init + start — this WILL break with TTL (2nd call is cached within window). (e) `test_init_initializes_mic_status_and_calls_probe` (line 1495) asserts `len(calls) == 1` after init — still passes if init uses force=True.
3. LOGIC: (a) Add a module-level constant near the top of daemon.py (after imports, before class defs): `_MIC_PROBE_TTL_S: float = 30.0  # re-probe mic health at most once every 30s (Issue 3: avoid ~40ms PyAudio init on every arm)`. (b) In __init__ (lines 453-455 area), add: `self._mic_probe_at: float = 0.0  # time.monotonic() of last probe; 0.0 == never (sentinel, matching MicRetryRateLimitFilter._last_seen)`. (c) Modify `_refresh_mic_status(self)` → `_refresh_mic_status(self, *, force: bool = False)`. Add a TTL gate at the TOP of the method body: `if not force and self._mic_probe_at != 0.0 and (time.monotonic() - self._mic_probe_at) < _MIC_PROBE_TTL_S: return`. This returns early (cached result stays valid) if the last probe was within the TTL window. After storing the probe result (after lines 646-647), stamp: `self._mic_probe_at = time.monotonic()`. (d) In __init__ (line 456), change `self._refresh_mic_status()` → `self._refresh_mic_status(force=True)` so construction always probes (sets the initial _mic_probe_at stamp). (e) _arm()'s call (line 581) stays `self._refresh_mic_status()` (no force) — it respects the TTL. (f) Update test_arm_refreshes_mic_status: within the TTL window (realtime monotonic, both calls happen within < 1s), assert `len(calls) == 1` (2nd arm is cached). OR if the test needs to prove re-probe after TTL expiry, use `_fixed_clock(monkeypatch, 0.0)` for construction, `_fixed_clock(monkeypatch, _MIC_PROBE_TTL_S + 1.0)` for the arm, and assert `len(calls) == 2`. Add a new test `test_mic_probe_cached_within_ttl`: construct with a counting prober (1 call, stamped at t=0), call d.start() at t=5.0 (within TTL) → assert still 1 call. Then call d.start() at t=35.0 (past TTL) → assert 2 calls. Use `_fixed_clock` for deterministic timestamps. IMPORTANT: _arm/_disarm are called under self._lock; the TTL fields are only accessed under the lock — do NOT add any locking.
4. OUTPUT: _arm() no longer re-probes PyAudio within the 30s TTL window. Single-user arm goes from ~40 ms to ~0 ms (cached). The mic-health surface (_mic_ok/_mic_error in voicectl status) stays current within 30s. Run: `.venv/bin/python -m pytest tests/test_daemon.py -v` (all 269+ tests pass, including updated + new TTL tests).
5. DOCS: [Mode A] Update the docstring of _refresh_mic_status() to document the TTL caching behavior (force param, _MIC_PROBE_TTL_S, the 0.0 sentinel convention). The _arm() docstring may need a note that mic probe is now TTL-cached. No external docs files — this is an internal performance optimization with no user-facing API change.

milestones.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
milestones.description: Final sweep of changeset-level documentation: README.md, tests/ACCEPTANCE.md, and any overview docs that summarize the whole delta. Per SOW §5 Mode B, this catches cross-cutting doc changes that only make sense once the whole changeset is in place.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation
tasks.description: Update README.md, tests/ACCEPTANCE.md, and any other overview docs to reflect the offline guarantee fix, the status.sh contract fix, and the mic probe TTL optimization. Verify the README's 'Fully-local voice typing' / 'nothing is sent to a cloud' claims are now accurate post-fix.

subtasks.title: Update README.md + ACCEPTANCE.md for the full changeset
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: This is the Mode B final-task documentation sweep (SOW §5). The implementing subtasks already handle per-file doc updates (Mode A: launch_daemon.sh comment, status.sh comment, install.sh summary line, daemon.py docstrings). This subtask handles the CROSS-CUTTING overview docs that span the whole changeset. README.md (referenced in PRD §1 as the source of the 'Fully-local voice typing' and 'nothing is sent to a cloud' claims) already states the right promises — the Issue 1 fix makes them TRUE. Verify they're accurate and consider adding a brief note about the offline enforcement mechanism (HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 in the launch wrapper). tests/ACCEPTANCE.md is the paste-target for acceptance evidence — update criterion 8 to reflect the non-circular offline proof.
2. INPUT: All implementing subtasks are complete (dependencies list all 6). README.md exists at repo root. tests/ACCEPTANCE.md exists in tests/. The changeset: (a) launch_daemon.sh now exports HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 + TRANSFORMERS_OFFLINE=1 (Issue 1); (b) status.sh now exits 0 (Issue 2); (c) mic probe is TTL-cached at 30s (Issue 3); (d) install.sh confirms offline operation (Issue 4); (e) new regression tests added.
3. LOGIC: (a) README.md: read the existing 'Fully-local voice typing' / 'nothing is sent to a cloud' lines (referenced as lines 3 & 6 in the PRD). Verify they're accurate post-fix. If they already state the right thing, no edit needed. If there's a features/capabilities section that should mention the offline enforcement, add a brief note (e.g. 'Offline mode enforced via HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 in the launch wrapper — models load from local cache, zero runtime network'). (b) tests/ACCEPTANCE.md: update criterion 8 evidence to reflect the non-circular offline proof (journal grep for zero HTTP requests after production-path start, not pre-set env vars). (c) Scan for any other overview docs (docs/, CONTRIBUTING.md, etc.) that reference offline behavior, status.sh, or mic health — update if stale. If no changes are needed, document why (the README already claims the right things and the fix validates them).
4. OUTPUT: README.md and tests/ACCEPTANCE.md are consistent with the deployed code. No stale claims. The changeset is fully documented.
5. DOCS: [Mode B] This IS the changeset-level documentation sync task. It touches README.md, tests/ACCEPTANCE.md, and any cross-cutting overview docs. It depends on ALL implementing subtasks and runs last.
